Metadata.Coram: Sundaresh Menon CJ; Chao Hick Tin JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Dynasty Line Limited

Facts.Text: Dynasty Line Limited acquired 29,537,367 shares in China Development Corporation Limited
Dynasty entered into sale and purchase agreements with several vendors for shares
Only 28% of the purchase price for the shares was paid by Dynasty
Dynasty pledged shares to Commerzbank as security for loans to Sia
Dynasty pledged shares to Creditanstalt Bankverein as security for loans to Sia
Sia was the sole shareholder of Dynasty Line Limited
Lee Howe Yong was a co-director of Dynasty Line Limited

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the directors of a company contravened their management obligations by pledging company assets when the company was insolvent or of doubtful solvency
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act in the best interests of the company, considering both shareholder and creditor interests, especially when the company is insolvent or nearing insolvency
Party_Details.Application: The directors acted against the company's interests by pledging its only asset for personal loans. the company was not insolvent at the time of the transactions
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the directors breached their duties by pledging the company's sole asset without benefiting the company, thereby compromising its ability to meet its obligations.

Metadata.Coram: Sundaresh Menon CJ; Chao Hick Tin JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Sukamto Sia

Facts.Text: Sukamto Sia was the sole shareholder of Dynasty Line Limited
On 23 April 1996, Sia caused Dynasty to pledge 12,032,302 CDC shares to Commerzbank (South East Asia) Limited as security for a loan facility to him
On 3 November 1997, Sia caused Dynasty to pledge 10,702,625 CDC shares to Creditanstalt Bankverein as security for a loan facility to him

Between April 1996 and November 1997, Sia caused Dynasty to pledge its shares to various financial institutions as security for loans to him, his business associate Franklin Syah, and Beswil Investment Pte Ltd
Sia knew that Dynasty had not fully paid for the shares and that a substantial amount of the purchase price remained outstanding
Sia was a director of Dynasty Line Limited

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director is legally responsible for compromising a company's financial position by using its assets for personal benefit
Party_Details.Rule: A director must not use company assets for personal gain if it jeopardizes the company's ability to meet its obligations
Party_Details.Application: The transactions were permissible under company law. the transactions were detrimental to the company's financial health
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court concluded that Sia breached his duties by using company assets for personal benefit, compromising the company's financial position.

Metadata.Coram: Sundaresh Menon CJ; Chao Hick Tin JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Sukamto Sia

Facts.Text: Sia claimed a collateral agreement existed allowing flexibility in payment terms.
Sia communicated with Low regarding payment flexibility.
Sia stated that Low agreed to flexible payment terms.
Sia referred to sale and purchase agreements with specified payment schedules.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a collateral agreement can modify the terms of a written contract regarding payment obligations
Party_Details.Rule: A collateral agreement must be clear and certain to modify the terms of a written contract
Party_Details.Application: A collateral agreement allowed for flexible payments. the existence of such an agreement
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence of a binding collateral agreement that modified the written contract terms.

Metadata.Coram: Sundaresh Menon CJ; Chao Hick Tin JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Low Tuck Kwong

Facts.Text: Low entered into sale and purchase agreements with Dynasty for shares

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a vendor is legally responsible for enforcing strict payment terms in the absence of a proven collateral agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A vendor is entitled to enforce the terms of a written contract unless a valid collateral agreement is proven
Party_Details.Application: The original contract terms were binding. for a collateral agreement allowing flexibility
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court upheld the original contract terms, finding no evidence of a collateral agreement.
